concerns.text: FR-006 states 'Constitution Principle VII is satisfied via effect sizes with confidence intervals for census data.' Constitution Principle VII (census-data exception) explicitly states 'p-values and confidence intervals are NOT required' for census data. This is a direct contradiction: the spec claims CIs are required for satisfaction while the Constitution says they are NOT required. Per maintainer amendment 2026-06-14, this must be resolved by removing 'with confidence intervals' from FR-006.
